title: Pouteria pubescens
taxonomy: Pouteria pubescens is classified within the family Sapotaceae. This family comprises trees and shrubs commonly known as sapotes or soapberries. The species Pouteria pubescens is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the genus Pouteria, which contains about 50 species. The classification of Pouteria pubescens reflects its evolutionary relationships within the Sapotaceae family.
uses: Pouteria pubescens is a species of plant with various traditional uses, particularly in folk medicine. It is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are often harvested for their medicinal properties, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In some parts of the world, Pouteria pubescens is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, its effectiveness and safety for these uses have not been extensively scientifically studied.

side_effects: Pouteria pubescens, also known as the Sabine Plum or Sabine Plum tree, is native to Mexico and Central America. However, information about its side effects is limited.
